name: Circular route through the fields of Báscones de Ojeda
name_original: Circular por los campos de Báscones de Ojeda
description: Hike through the fields of Báscones de Ojeda on this easy 4.3-mile route, passing the historic Church of San Bartolomé.
region_intro: Hiking around Boedo-Ojeda offers diverse landscapes in the province of Palencia, Spain. The region serves as a transitional zone, featuring gentle valleys, rolling hills, and more rugged mountainous areas. It is characterized by extensive oak and pine forests, numerous river basins, and the historic Canal de Castilla, which provides flat towpaths and ecological corridors. This varied topography integrates natural exploration with a rich Romanesque heritage. Best hiking trails around Boedo-Ojeda The most popular hiking route is Fuente la Gallina y Mojón de la Abadesa, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the source of the Canal de Castilla. Another top favourite among local hikers is Centro de Interpretación del Canal de Castella, an easy 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This circular route from Herrera de Pisuerga follows the Canal de Castilla, offering views of its historical structures. Local hikers also love the Santibáñez de Ecla y Peña Pico, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Peña Pico hill, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around Boedo-Ojeda is defined by the Canal de Castilla, rolling hills, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy canal-side walks to more challenging routes through hills and fields. The routes in Boedo-Ojeda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 100 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Boedo-Ojeda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes recorded in Boedo-Ojeda, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The komoot community has explored these diverse trails more than 150 times.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: Yes, Boedo-Ojeda offers several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. The region's defining feature, the Canal de Castilla, provides many flat and accessible towpaths. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067701541/centro-de-interpretacion-del-canal-de-castella-circular-desde-herrera-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centro de Interpretación del Canal de Castella circular route&lt;/a&gt; from Herrera de Pisuerga, which is 4.8 miles (7.7 km) long and follows the Canal, showcasing its historical structures.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: Boedo-Ojeda offers a diverse range of landscapes, acting as a transitional zone between the plains of Tierra de Campos and the rugged Montaña Palentina. You'll encounter gentle valleys, rolling hills, extensive oak and pine forests, and numerous river basins. The historic Canal de Castilla also provides unique waterside scenery and wetlands.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: Yes, many routes in Boedo-Ojeda are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309682863/fuente-la-gallina-y-mojon-de-la-abadesa-circular-desde-alar-del-rey-por-el-nacimiento-del-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente la Gallina y Mojón de la Abadesa&lt;/a&gt; route near the source of the Canal de Castilla, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314430551/santibanez-de-ecla-y-pena-pico-circular-desde-pradanos-de-ojeda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santibáñez de Ecla y Peña Pico&lt;/a&gt; trail, which explores varied terrain.

Q: What unique historical or cultural sites can I explore on a hike in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: Boedo-Ojeda is rich in Romanesque heritage, with many hiking routes passing by ancient religious buildings. You can discover sites like the Monasterio de San Andrés del Arroyo, the Church of Santa Eufemia de Cozuelos, and the Ermita de San Pelayo. The famous Church of San Martín de Tours in Frómista, a significant Romanesque landmark, is also connected to the Canal de Castilla.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: The hiking trails in Boedo-Ojeda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique blend of natural beauty along the Canal de Castilla with the rich Romanesque heritage, as well as the diverse landscapes ranging from forests to rolling hills.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, several notable attractions can be found along or near the hiking trails. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5831684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dock and Locks of the Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6122552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Andrés Sluice&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4918406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Museum&lt;/a&gt; in Herrera de Pisuerga is also a popular stop.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: The diverse topography of Boedo-Ojeda makes it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, ideal for exploring the forests and canal paths. Even in winter, some of the flatter canal-side routes can be enjoyable, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking more challenge, Boedo-Ojeda offers routes with greater distances and elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067732054/castrillo-de-riopisuerga-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrillo de Riopisuerga circular route&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 14.5-mile (23.4 km) trail along the Canal de Castilla. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Andrés Suspension Bridge – Herrera de Pisuerga Chapel loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 13.5 miles (21.8 km).

Q: Can I combine hiking with other activities in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: Absolutely. The Canal de Castilla, a central feature of the region, is not only great for hiking but also offers opportunities for boat tours on certain navigable sections. Additionally, the historic towns like Herrera de Pisuerga provide cultural points of interest, allowing you to blend outdoor activity with historical exploration.

Q: Is it possible to hike through forests in Boedo-Ojeda?
A: Yes, Boedo-Ojeda is characterized by extensive oak and pine forests, providing ample opportunities for forest hikes. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314447436/pena-pico-from-the-monasterio-de-san-andres-de-arroyo-de-santibanez-de-ecla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Pico from the Monasterio de San Andrés de Arroyo&lt;/a&gt; lead through wooded areas and offer scenic views.
tips: Palencia town located in the upper part of the Boedo river dividing the town into two neighborhoods. Its name comes from the times of the Reconquest and repopulation of people from Vasconia or the Kingdom of Navarre in the 9th century.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This easy hike through the fields of Báscones de Ojeda typically takes around 1 hour and 52 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This trail is classified as easy and is ideal for beginners, families, and individuals of any fitness level. The paths are easily accessible, making it a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Báscones de Ojeda is a small village. You can generally find parking within the village itself, close to the starting point of the route. Look for available spaces near the village center or the Church of San Bartolomé.

faqs.question: Is the Hike through the fields of Báscones de Ojeda dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. Given its rural setting and easy terrain, it's a great option for walking with your dog. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike through the fields of Báscones de Ojeda is typically during the spring or autumn months. During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, and the fields are vibrant with either spring blooms or autumn colors. Summer can also be enjoyable, but it's advisable to start early to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by its rural setting, leading you through expansive fields. You'll encounter easily accessible paths across gentle valleys and rolling hills, typical of the Boedo-Ojeda region. It's a relatively flat and straightforward walk.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant landmark along the route is the Church of San Bartolomé of Báscones de Ojeda, an important religious site in the area. The trail itself offers classic rural Spanish landscapes with vast agricultural vistas and natural elements.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the fields of Báscones de Ojeda. This is a public access trail, freely open for everyone to enjoy.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the rural and agricultural setting, you might spot various types of birds, small mammals, and insects common to Spanish farmlands and natural areas. Keep an eye out for local flora and fauna as you traverse the fields.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this easy hike, comfortable walking shoes or light hiking boots are recommended. Dress in layers suitable for the season, and bring water, especially on warmer days. Sun protection like a hat and sunscreen is also advisable, as much of the trail is exposed to the sun.

faqs.question: Is this a circular walk, or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The "Hike through the fields of Báscones de Ojeda" is designed as a circular route, meaning you will end up back near your starting point without having to retrace your steps. This allows you to experience a continuous loop of the beautiful rural landscape.

waypoints.name: Church of San Bartolomé of Báscones de Ojeda
